steps.title: Asthma
steps.text: What is the recommended target oxygen saturation level for children with mild asthma at the primary care level?
steps.rationale: The guideline clearly states that the target oxygen saturation for managing mild asthma at the primary level is between 94-98%. Lower targets are used for other conditions like chronic lung disease; higher targets (>99%) may lead to unnecessary oxygen use.

steps.title: Asthma
steps.text: When treating mild asthma at the primary level, which of the following is a correct initial management option?
steps.rationale: The guideline recommends Salbutamol inhaler via spacer (100-200 mcg, 1-2 puffs) every 20 minutes for 1 hour as the first-line treatment. Nebulisation is also an option but at more frequent intervals (every 15 mins for 3 doses). Antibiotics and antihistamines are not primary treatments for asthma exacerbations.

textChoices.text: Salbutamol inhaler via spacer every 20 minutes for 1 hour

steps.title: Asthma
steps.text: According to the guidelines, which of the following advice should be provided on discharge after managing mild asthma?
steps.rationale: The discharge advice in the guideline includes: checking inhaler and spacer technique, avoiding allergens, returning if symptoms worsen, and possibly adding a daily inhaled steroid if frequent episodes occur. Mild cases do not need referral to tertiary care unless they worsen.

textChoices.text: Stop all medications immediately if no symptoms
Avoid allergens and check inhaler technique
Refer all mild cases to tertiary hospital
Only use oral medications at home
